docs.title: Nexus
docs.text:
Nexus is a lightweight Roblox networking module that simplifies remote communication between the server and clients.

AI-Assisted Documentation

Documentation is AI-Assisted and may contain inaccuracies. Please report any issues or suggestions on the GitHub Issues Page.

It ships with three main abstractions:

  • Event — reliable fire-and-forget remote events.
  • Async — request-response remote functions.
  • UnreliableEvent — fire-and-forget events for non-critical real-time updates.

docs.title: What is Nexus for?
docs.text: Nexus is designed for Roblox developers who want a clean, organized pattern for declaring and using remote channels without manually managing RemoteEvent, RemoteFunction, and UnreliableRemoteEvent instances.

docs.title: Documentation
docs.text:
• Getting Started
  • API Reference
  • Development

docs.title: Local development
docs.text:
• Use Rojo to build and sync the project.
  • Use Wally to install dependencies and keep versions consistent.

docs.title: Recommended workflow
docs.text:
wally install
rojo build -o Nexus.rbxlx
rojo serve

docs.title: Running documentation locally
docs.text:
Install MkDocs and build the site:

python -m pip install mkdocs
mkdocs serve

Then open the local server URL shown in the terminal.

docs.title: Repository layout
docs.text:
• src/ — Luau source files for Nexus.
  • docs/ — MkDocs documentation source files.
  • mkdocs.yml — MkDocs site configuration.
  • wally.toml — Wally package metadata.
  • default.project.json — Rojo project definition.

docs.title: Contribution
docs.text: If you want to contribute, please open a pull request with a clear description of your changes. Keep API additions compatible with the existing event and async abstractions.

docs.title: Requirements
docs.text:
• Roblox Studio
  • Rojo (for local development and project syncing)
  • A Roblox place or game to load your Nexus module into

docs.title: Installing Nexus
docs.text:
Nexus is packaged with Wally and can be included as a dependency in your Roblox project.

If you use Wally, add Nexus to your dependency list in wally.toml and install it with:

wally install

Alternatively, copy the src folder into your project and require src/init.

docs.title: Basic Workflow
docs.text:
1. Create your networking definition using Nexus.Define().
  2. Call Nexus.Start() on the server to initialize the remote instances.
  3. Register event handlers, then use the Fire and Invoke methods from client and server code.

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
local Nexus = require(path.to.Nexus)

local remotes = Nexus.Define({
    chat = {
        playerMessage = Nexus.Event(),
        requestStatus = Nexus.Async(),
        heartbeat = Nexus.UnreliableEvent(),
    }
})

-- Server setup
if game:GetService("RunService"):IsServer() then
    Nexus.Start()

    remotes.chat.playerMessage:OnServerEvent(function(player, message)
        print(player.Name .. " says: " .. message)
    end)

    remotes.chat.requestStatus:OnServerInvoke(function(player)
        return {
            time = os.time(),
            players = #game:GetService("Players"):GetPlayers(),
        }
    end)
end

-- Client usage
if game:GetService("RunService"):IsClient() then
    remotes.chat.playerMessage:FireServer("Hello from the client!")

    local status = remotes.chat.requestStatus:InvokeServer()
    print(status.time)
end

docs.title: When to use each type
docs.text:
• Use Event for reliable broadcast-style communication.
  • Use Async when you need a response from the remote side.
  • Use UnreliableEvent for fast updates where dropped packets are acceptable.

docs.title: Async
docs.text: Async provides request-response behavior using Roblox RemoteFunction semantics.

docs.title: Async:OnServerInvoke(callback)
docs.text:
Registers a callback on the server for client invocations.

  • callback(player, ...) — receives the calling player and any arguments.

docs.title: Async:OnClientInvoke(callback)
docs.text:
Registers a callback on the client for server invocations.

  • callback(...) — receives any forwarded arguments.

docs.title: Async:InvokeServer(...)
docs.text: Invokes the server from the client and returns the server response.

docs.title: Async:InvokeClient(player, ...)
docs.text: Invokes a specific client from the server and returns the client response.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text: Use Async when the remote side must answer with a value before continuing.

docs.title: Event
docs.text: The Event object provides a reliable remote event channel for server and client communication.

docs.title: Event:OnServerEvent(callback)
docs.text:
Registers a callback on the server that runs when a client fires the event.

  • callback(player, ...) — receives the player who fired the event and the forwarded arguments.

docs.title: Event:OnClientEvent(callback)
docs.text:
Registers a callback on the client that runs when the server fires the event.

  • callback(...) — receives the forwarded arguments.

docs.title: Event:FireServer(...)
docs.text: Fires the event from the client to the server.

docs.title: Event:FireClient(player, ...)
docs.text: Fires the event from the server to a single client.

docs.title: Event:FireAllClientsExcept(ignorePlayer, ...)
docs.text: Fires the event to every client except the ignored player.

docs.title: Event:FireAllClients(...)
docs.text: Fires the event to every connected client.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text: Use Event for reliable messages that must arrive in order and should not be dropped.

docs.title: Nexus API
docs.text: The root Nexus module is responsible for defining named remote channels and starting the underlying Roblox remote instances.

docs.title: Nexus.Event()
docs.text:
Creates a new reliable event channel.

Returns:

  • Event — a new event instance.

docs.title: Nexus.Async()
docs.text:
Creates a new request-response async channel.

Returns:

  • Async — a new async instance.

docs.title: Nexus.UnreliableEvent()
docs.text:
Creates a new unreliable event channel.

Returns:

  • UnreliableEvent — a new unreliable event instance.

docs.title: Nexus.Define(remotes)
docs.text:
Registers a collection of named remote channels to be used by the module.

Parameters:

  • remotes — a table shaped like { namespace = { channelName = Nexus.Event() | Nexus.Async() | Nexus.UnreliableEvent() } }.

Returns:

  • The same remotes table with network identifiers assigned.

docs.title: Nexus.Start()
docs.text:
Initializes the underlying RemoteEvent, RemoteFunction, and UnreliableRemoteEvent instances on the server, and attaches client-side handlers on the client.

Call Nexus.Start() once before using defined remote channels in your game.

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• Define must be called before the first network request.
  • Start must be called on the server before client code attempts to use the remote channels.

docs.title: UnreliableEvent
docs.text: The UnreliableEvent object uses Roblox UnreliableRemoteEvent to send fast, best-effort messages.

docs.title: UnreliableEvent:OnServerEvent(callback)
docs.text:
Registers a callback on the server that runs when a client fires the unreliable event.

  • callback(player, ...) — receives the player and any arguments.

docs.title: UnreliableEvent:OnClientEvent(callback)
docs.text:
Registers a callback on the client that runs when the server fires the unreliable event.

  • callback(...) — receives the forwarded arguments.

docs.title: UnreliableEvent:FireServer(...)
docs.text: Fires the event from the client to the server.

docs.title: UnreliableEvent:FireClient(player, ...)
docs.text: Fires the event from the server to a specific client.

docs.title: UnreliableEvent:FireAllClientsExcept(ignorePlayer, ...)
docs.text: Fires the event to every client except the ignored player.

docs.title: UnreliableEvent:FireAllClients(...)
docs.text: Fires the event to every connected client.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text: Use UnreliableEvent for high-frequency updates, telemetry, or other messages where occasional packet loss is acceptable.
